name: How much does structured data generation usually cost, and what am I actually paying for?

mainEntity.name: How much does structured data generation usually cost, and what am I actually paying for?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Costs for structured data generation range from nearly zero (DIY scripts and free validators) to significant monthly fees for enterprise platforms or consulting, depending on complexity and scale.
For many small and mid‑size sites, the main cost is implementation time rather than tools.

Key factors:
- Number of page types and domains you’re marking up
- Need for synthetic data vs. just SEO markup
- Internal engineering and SEO capacity
- Ongoing maintenance and monitoring requirements

Practically, budget a few days of developer/SEO time to set up basic JSON-LD for key templates, then consider paid tools only if you need automation across thousands of pages or complex synthetic datasets.
Ask vendors for a clear breakdown of implementation effort, license fees, and maintenance overhead.
If budget is tight, start with the 10–20% of pages that drive most of your traffic or revenue.
acceptedAnswer.description: Costs range from free (DIY with JSON-LD and validators) to substantial monthly fees for enterprise platforms or consultants. You’re mainly paying for schema design, implementation across templates, automation for large sites or synthetic datasets, and ongoing monitoring/maintenance of data quality.
